_note: Schema + file structure for #35 Skill Telemetry. The live ledger lives at `.flutter-pipeline/telemetry.json` (target app root), NOT here — this file documents its shape. Local only, no PII, no source code. Read by #30 Token_Budget_Governor (cost_model) and #28 Skill_Router (routing priors).

$schema_record._doc: One object per skill invocation. Append exactly one after each run finishes. Canonical token fields are tokens_in/tokens_out; input/output are MIRRORS so #30's estimate.py (which reads r['input']/r['output']) consumes the log unmodified.

skill_id.doc: Full pipeline key matching #30 cost_model.json, e.g. '06_Flutter_Architecture'.

ts.doc: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the run finished, e.g. '2026-06-23T18:04:11Z'.

outcome.doc: success = gate passed first try (rework_count 0); rework = passed after redo(s); fail = abandoned/blocked.

tokens_in.doc: Input tokens from response usage (include cache reads if known). >= 0.

tokens_out.doc: Output tokens from usage; thinking tokens bill as output so count them. >= 0.

input.doc: MIRROR of tokens_in for #30 compatibility. Write the same value.

output.doc: MIRROR of tokens_out for #30 compatibility. Write the same value.

rework_count.doc: Number of redo loops before the run passed; 0 for a clean first pass.

notes.doc: Short, mechanical, optional. NO PII, NO source code, NO prompts/user text. e.g. 'gate failed once on analyzer'.

$schema_file._doc: The live telemetry.json is a single object: header keys + `skills` map of skill_id -> array of records. #30 reads obj['skills'][skill_id] as a list. A flat top-level array of records is also tolerated by rollup.py, but the grouped shape below is preferred.

$example._note: Local performance ledger. No PII, no source. Written by #35, read by #30/#28.

06_Flutter_Architecture.notes: gate failed once on analyzer warning
